spec_match.rationale: The demo now models the actual uniform_buffer_standard_layout contracts: array stride, nested-struct offsets, and the important vec3 case where alignment does not shrink.

interactive.rationale: Visitors can choose scenarios, change values and array counts, compare live offset tables, and click individual bytes to inspect ownership and stored values.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The lede now matches the feature framing by calling out storage-buffer-compatible uniform constraints, array alignment, nested-struct padding, and vec3 assumptions.

edge_cases.rationale: The page surfaces missing navigator.gpu, missing wgslLanguageFeatures support, no-adapter/device failures, shader compile errors, and no-saving vec3 scenarios.

references.rationale: The concept page directly links to https://chromestatus.com/feature/6680245553987584 in its see-also section.

design_system.rationale: The page uses shared CSS variables for colors, keeps button background and foreground paired, and preserves the existing stage and panel visual language.

summary: vec3-trap under webgpu-uniform-buffer-standard-layout now clears the critique: the static diagram became an interactive byte layout workbench with WebGPU capability messaging and the required ChromeStatus reference.
